metadata.title_summary:
title: Deliveries by model type 
summary: In Q4 2023, vehicle sales surged to 3,749 units, a remarkable 110% increase from Q3's 1,782 units, primarily driven by a 191% rise in lifestyle vehicle sales, which jumped from 891 to 2,599 units. Sportscar sales also increased by 29%, from 891 units in Q3 to 1,150 in Q4. For the entire year, total vehicle sales reached 6,970 units, with lifestyle vehicles accounting for 4,361 units and sportscars for 2,609 units.

The "Regional Deliveries Comparison" highlights significant growth in deliveries across various regions from December 31, 2022, to December 31, 2023. North America saw deliveries rise from 41 to 47, Europe from 54 to 70, and China from 41 to 65, while deliveries in the Rest of the World remained steady at 33. Overall, total deliveries increased from 169 to 215, showcasing the company's successful expansion efforts.

Financially, Lotus Tech reported substantial growth in revenue and gross profit in 2023, particularly in Q4, where revenue reached $361 million, a 92% increase from Q3's $188 million. The total revenue for the year was $679 million. However, the cost of sales also rose to $293 million in Q4, leading to a gross profit of $68 million, up 142% from Q3. Despite these gains, the company faced significant operating and net losses, with an operating loss of $228 million in Q4 and a total net loss of $750 million for the year. Adjusted EBITDA also worsened, reflecting ongoing challenges in executing its Vision80 strategy amidst financial pressures.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Lotus Technology recorded revenue of \$679 million in 2023, driven by growing sales of lifestyle battery electric vehicles (BEVs) and sports cars. The company achieved a gross profit margin of 15% in the first year of BEV deliveries, with the scaling of asset-light production. Lotus Technology expanded its global footprint to 215 stores, with new flagship locations in Paris, London, and Seoul. The company expects to achieve about a threefold increase in deliveries in 2024 amid steady market expansion. New York - April 8, 2024 - Lotus Technology Inc. ("Lotus Tech" or "the Company"), a leading global luxury electric vehicle maker, today announced its unaudited financial results for the fourth quarter and the year ended December 31, 2023. The results reflect steady progress in Lotus Technology's execution of its Vision80 strategy to become an all-electric, intelligent, and luxury mobility provider before Lotus Technology's 80th anniversary in 2028. In its first year of battery electric vehicle (BEV) delivery, Lotus Technology achieved revenue of \$679 million and a gross profit margin of 15% in 2023. Revenue in the fourth quarter of 2023 was \$361 million, representing a 92% quarter-on-quarter increase. This strong performance was enabled by Lotus Technology's asset-light business model, as the company ramped up production and sales of BEV models. Lotus Technology delivered 6,970 vehicles in 2023, an annual record in the 76-year history of the Lotus brand, with battery electric vehicle (BEV) models representing 63% of total deliveries. Vehicle deliveries in the fourth quarter of 2023 increased nearly 110% quarter-over-quarter to 3,749.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Lotus Technology Reports Unaudited Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2023 Financial Results 
summary: In 2023, Lotus Tech achieved significant milestones, reporting a revenue of $679 million, driven by strong sales of lifestyle battery electric vehicles (BEVs) and sports cars. The company recorded a gross profit margin of 15% in its inaugural year of BEV deliveries, benefiting from an asset-light production model. Lotus Tech expanded its global footprint to 215 stores, including new flagship locations in major cities like Paris, London, and Seoul, and delivered a record 6,970 vehicles, with BEVs making up 63% of total deliveries. The fourth quarter alone saw a remarkable 92% increase in revenue, reaching $361 million, and nearly 110% growth in vehicle deliveries compared to the previous quarter, largely due to the success of the Eletre lifestyle BEV.

Looking forward, Lotus Tech anticipates a nearly threefold increase in deliveries in 2024, targeting a total of 26,000 units while aiming to enhance its gross margin to between 17-19%. The company is set to expand its Vision80 strategy, which seeks to transform Lotus into a fully electric and luxury mobility provider by 2028. Deliveries of the Emeya, its first electric hyper-grand tourer, have begun in China, with European deliveries expected in the third quarter. CEO Qingfeng Feng expressed optimism about the company's progress, emphasizing a commitment to long-term growth and value creation for customers and investors as they leverage their luxury brand and advanced technology.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: The increase in deliveries was driven primarily by the Eletre, Lotus Technology's first lifestyle BEV, which ramped up production and sales in the second half of the year. Lotus Technology also continued to expand its global presence, laying the foundation for the ongoing global roll-out of the brand's vehicles with an increase of 46 stores year-on-year to a total of 215 locations as of December 31, 2023. Over the past year, Lotus Technology notably opened new flagship stores in prime locations, including in the Golden Triangle area of Paris, Mayfair in London, Gangnam-gu in Seoul, and other strategically important global cities. 2024 will be a pivotal year for Lotus Technology Inc.'s execution of its Vision80 Strategy, as the company further ramps up global deliveries of its leading product portfolio in new markets. In March 2024, the company began deliveries of the Emeya, its first electric hyper-grand tourer. Emeya deliveries are now underway in China and are expected to begin in Europe in the third quarter of 2024. Lotus Technology's Eletre hyper-SUV is also set to enter more markets, including the U.S., over the course of 2024. In 2024, the company expects to increase its total deliveries about threefold to 26,000 and further grow its healthy gross margin to 17-19%. "Lotus Technology is pleased with the early progress and promising results achieved in 2023, with increasing deliveries reflecting the strength of the brand and ramping production," said Lotus Tech CEO Qingfeng Feng.

summary: Lotus Technology Inc. announced its preliminary vehicle delivery results for the first nine months of 2024 on October 17, 2024, reporting a total of 7,543 vehicles delivered, which represents a remarkable 134% increase compared to the same period last year. The deliveries were well-distributed across global markets, with Europe, China, North America, and the Rest of the World each contributing approximately 20-35% to the overall delivery figures.

In the third quarter of 2024, the company successfully delivered the Emeya (GT, Sedan) in Europe, while the Eletre (SUV) expanded its reach to markets including the Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC), Japan, and Korea. Notably, the Eletre Carbon, a high-performance variant specifically designed for the North American market, was revealed in September 2024, with a starting price set at $229,900. Deliveries for this model are anticipated to commence in the first half of 2025. Additionally, bespoke and collection versions of both the Eletre and Emeya began their deliveries in Q3 2024.

Lotus also showcased the Theory 1 concept car in September, highlighting its commitment to advanced technology and eco-friendly features. It is important to note that the delivery figures provided are based on reports from business units and may differ from those recorded in financial accounting systems. This comprehensive overview underscores Lotus Technology's significant growth trajectory and its strategic expansion into various international markets.

metadata.title_summary:
title: A PIVOTAL YEAR DELIVERING LOTUS'VISION8O STRATEGY 
summary: In 2023, Lotus made significant strides in its transition to electric mobility, with the BEV Lifestyle model representing 63% of total deliveries in its first year. The brand expanded its global footprint to 215 stores, a 46-location increase from the previous year, supporting the Vision80 strategy alongside a balanced distribution network. The appointment of nichLI as the exclusive global distributor further bolstered its market presence. 

The year also marked the 75th anniversary of Lotus, celebrated with the launch of the Eletre, the brand's first all-electric hyper SUV, during an event that included a parade of over 600 Lotus drivers at an F1 racetrack, showcasing the company’s heritage and future in electric vehicles.

Financially, Lotus reported impressive growth metrics for 4Q23 and FY2023. Deliveries surged by 110% in 4Q23 to 3,749 units, culminating in a total of 6,970 units for FY2023, a remarkable leap from just 7 units in FY2022. Revenue for 4Q23 reached $361 million, a 92% increase from the previous quarter, with FY2023 revenue soaring to $679 million from $9 million in FY2022. 

Despite rising costs, with 4Q23 cost of sales at $293 million (up 83% QoQ) and FY2023 totaling $577 million, gross profit also saw substantial growth, reaching $68 million in 4Q23 and $102 million for the year. The gross profit margin improved to 19% in 4Q23, while net losses for the quarter and the year were $(224) million and $(750) million, respectively, reflecting ongoing investments in growth.

Looking ahead to FY2024, Lotus anticipates continued growth in deliveries and advancements in its evolution as an intelligent luxury mobility provider. The financial metrics for FY2022 are considered "not comparable" due to the initiation of BEV deliveries in 2023, and EBIT and adjusted EBITDA figures are provided as non-GAAP measures with further details available in the appendix.

Total revenue reached $653 million, more than doubling year-on-year. Revenue from the intelligent driving business generated from customers other than Lotus surged to $11 million, with a year-on-year growth of 450%. Lotus Technology launched Hyper Hybrid EV technology that provides high performance with a combined range of over 1,100 kilometers. New York – Nov 21, 2024 – Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus Tech” or the “Company”), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its unaudited financial results for the third quarter and first nine months ended September 30, 2024.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Lotus Technology Reports Unaudited Third
Quarter and First Nine Months 2024 Financial Results 
summary: Lotus Technology Inc. has released its unaudited financial results for the third quarter and the first nine months of 2024, concluding on September 30, 2024. During this period, the company achieved a remarkable milestone by delivering over 7,600 vehicles, which represents a substantial year-on-year increase of 136%. Total revenue for the company reached $653 million, more than doubling compared to the same period last year. 

A significant highlight of the financial results was the performance of the intelligent driving business, which generated $11 million in revenue from customers other than Lotus, showcasing an impressive year-on-year growth of 450%. 

In addition to these financial achievements, Lotus Technology announced the launch of its Hyper Hybrid EV technology on November 21, 2024. This innovative technology promises high performance with a combined driving range exceeding 1,100 kilometers, further solidifying the company's commitment to advancing electric vehicle technology.

Overall, Lotus Technology's third-quarter and nine-month results reflect strong growth and innovation within the company, positioning it favorably in the competitive automotive market.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Operating and Financial Results for the Second
Quarter of 2024 
summary: In the second quarter of 2024, the company achieved notable growth, delivering a total of 2,679 units, which represents a remarkable 128% increase compared to the same period in 2023. This growth was reflected in total revenues of $225 million, up 103% from $111 million a year earlier, alongside an improved gross margin of 9%, compared to 5% in Q2 2023. Despite these positive trends, the company reported a net loss of $202 million, a 4% increase from the previous year's loss, and an operating loss of $204 million, which also rose by 12%.

Breaking down the deliveries by model type, the "Lifestyle SUV and Sedan" category saw significant growth, with 1,342 units delivered, up from 637 units in Q2 2023, marking an increase of 111%. Similarly, "Sportscars" deliveries reached 1,337 units, a substantial rise from 538 units, reflecting a 149% year-over-year increase. 

Adjusted figures showed an adjusted net loss of $201 million and an adjusted EBITDA loss of $177 million, indicating a slight improvement from the previous year. The cost of revenues increased significantly to $204 million, highlighting the challenges the company continues to face despite overall growth. An earnings conference call is scheduled for August 28, 2024, to discuss these results in detail. All financial figures are rounded and unaudited.

Lastly, the Type 135 is an all-electric sports car, Lotus's first BEV sports model, expected to launch in 2025 with deliveries in 2027, priced above $95,000 and projected to sell between 10,000 and 15,000 units annually starting in 2029.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Certain Prospective Operational and Financial Information 
summary: On November 28, 2022, Lotus Tech provided LCAA with initial financial projections for 2023 to 2025, including revenue, gross profit, and EBITDA margin estimates, to assist in their due diligence process prior to the approval of a business combination. These projections were updated in June and October 2023, collectively referred to as the "Financial Projections." It is important to note that these projections were not prepared in accordance with SEC guidelines or GAAP and were intended for internal use, reflecting management's expectations and assumptions about future performance, which may be subject to significant uncertainties.

The Financial Projections indicate a strong growth trajectory, with projected revenues for 2023 ranging from approximately $2.2 billion to $2.5 billion, increasing to $6.2 billion to $6.6 billion in 2024, and reaching $8.5 billion to $8.9 billion by 2025. Gross profit is expected to rise from $0.3 billion to $0.4 billion in 2023 to $1.8 billion to $2.0 billion by 2025, while gross margins are projected to improve from 14.0%–16.0% in 2023 to 21.0%–23.0% in 2025.

Lotus Tech anticipates selling over 76,000 vehicles by 2025, driven by several key models, including the Eletre and Type 133. The company expects significant contributions from vehicle sales, technology-related revenue, and ecosystem services. However, the projections carry inherent risks, including potential delays in vehicle launches and market dynamics, which could lead to discrepancies between projected and actual results.

Lotus Tech emphasizes that the Financial Projections should not be relied upon as definitive guidance, as they are speculative and subject to numerous uncertainties. The company does not guarantee the accuracy of these forecasts and will not reference them in future reports post-business combination. The projections were prepared by management and have not been audited by KPMG Huazhen LLP, which only provided assurance on historical financial information. Overall, while the projections suggest a promising outlook for Lotus Tech, stakeholders are advised to approach them with caution due to the various risks and assumptions involved.

summary: On November 28, 2022, Lotus Tech provided LCAA with initial financial forecasts covering revenue, gross profit, and EBITDA margin estimates for 2023 to 2025, which were prepared without adherence to SEC guidelines or U.S. GAAP. Lotus Tech emphasizes that these projections are subjective and based on various assumptions, many of which are uncertain and may change over time, potentially leading to significant discrepancies between projected and actual results. The company does not publicly disclose such forecasts and does not intend to do so in the future, highlighting that the projections should not be relied upon as guarantees of future performance.

The Financial Projections indicate a growth trajectory, with revenues expected to rise from approximately $2.2 billion to $2.5 billion in 2023, reaching between $6.2 billion and $6.6 billion in 2024, and further increasing to between $8.5 billion and $8.9 billion in 2025. Gross profit is projected to grow from $0.3 billion to $0.4 billion in 2023 to between $1.8 billion and $2.0 billion by 2025, with gross margins improving from 14%–16% to 21%–23% over the same period.

The company also projects growth in ecosystem-related revenue through charging services and peripheral product sales. However, stakeholders are cautioned about the inherent risks and uncertainties that could impact these forecasts, including market conditions, competitive pressures, and operational challenges.

Overall, while Lotus Tech's management has outlined optimistic projections based on reasonable assumptions, the company acknowledges the potential for actual results to vary significantly from these estimates, underscoring the need for caution among investors and stakeholders.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Overview 
summary: We are a leading global provider of intelligent and luxury mobility solutions, specializing in the design and development of luxury lifestyle vehicles under the esteemed British brand "Lotus." With a rich racing heritage spanning over 70 years, Lotus is renowned for its high-performance standards in both design and engineering. Since Geely Holding acquired a 51% stake in Lotus UK in 2017, we have successfully transitioned into a luxury lifestyle battery electric vehicle (BEV) manufacturer, benefiting from Geely's support across various operational areas.

Our flagship model, the Eletre, is a luxury E-segment SUV that showcases our innovative 800-volt Electrical Performance Architecture (EPA). The Eletre is available in three versions: Eletre, Eletre S, and Eletre R. The Eletre R stands out with an impressive 905 horsepower, achieving acceleration from 0 to 100 km/h in just 2.95 seconds, and offers a WLTP range of 490 km, complemented by rapid charging capabilities. Deliveries of the Eletre commenced in 2023, and we launched our second model, the Emeya, in September 2023, with deliveries set to begin in March 2024. Furthermore, we have plans to introduce two additional models in the near future.

Our research and development capabilities, deeply rooted in Lotus's sports car legacy, serve as a significant competitive advantage, exemplified by the five-year development of the EPA, which facilitates rapid model rollout and enhances production efficiency. Additionally, we have developed an advanced Advanced Driver Assistance System (ADAS) featuring Level 4-ready hardware and a deployable LiDAR system.

To enhance customer experience, we employ a digital-first, omni-channel sales model, operating premium stores in high-traffic areas alongside a global digital platform for vehicle customization and purchasing. As of June 30, 2024, our global distribution network comprises 207 stores. In 2023, we delivered a total of 6,970 vehicles, with 4,873 vehicles delivered in the first half of 2024. Our workforce consists of 2,989 full-time employees, with a substantial 54.6% dedicated to research and development. 

This comprehensive approach positions us at the forefront of the luxury electric vehicle market, driving innovation and excellence in mobility solutions.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Lotus Technology is a luxury battery electric vehicle (BEV) maker that designs, develops, and sells luxury lifestyle vehicles under the iconic British brand “Lotus.” With over seven decades of heritage and proven leadership in racetrack and road cars, the Lotus brand symbolizes the market-leading standards in performance, design, and engineering. Lotus Technology's first lifestyle production vehicle, the Eletre, is a new breed of pure electric SUV powered by Lotus’s proprietary 800-volt EPA. Deliveries of the Eletre commenced in China and Europe in 2023. Planning is underway for deliveries to the United States. U.S. and rest of the world. Lotus Technology's second lifestyle production vehicle, Emeya, was launched in September 2023, and Lotus Technology began deliveries of Emeya in March 2024. In addition to the Eletre and Emeya, Lotus Technology plans to launch two additional fully-electric vehicles in the near future, including a D-segment SUV in 2025 and a BEV sports car in 2026. Lotus Technology also plans to upgrade its models on an ongoing basis. Lotus Technology's first lifestyle production vehicle, the Eletre, is a new breed of pure electric SUV powered by Lotus’s proprietary 800-volt EPA. Deliveries of the Eletre commenced in China and Europe in 2023. Planning is underway for deliveries to the United States. The Eletre is the first of Lotus Technology's new breed of pure electric SUVs. The Eletre is Lotus Technology's first lifestyle vehicle, which aims to set the standard for future lifestyle vehicles.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Our Vehicles 
summary: Lotus, a prestigious British luxury battery electric vehicle (BEV) manufacturer with over 70 years of expertise in high-performance automotive design, is redefining the electric vehicle market with its innovative lifestyle offerings. The company's first production vehicle, the Eletre, is a state-of-the-art all-electric SUV that showcases advanced technology and performance, featuring a proprietary 800-volt EPA powertrain. Launched in 2023, the Eletre combines a sleek aerodynamic design with a luxurious interior and cutting-edge connectivity features, including a 15.1-inch OLED display and 5G compatibility for real-time updates. It offers three distinct versions—Eletre, Eletre S, and Eletre R—catering to varying customer preferences, with impressive acceleration and range capabilities.

Following the Eletre, Lotus introduced the Emeya, a four-door luxury hyper grand tourer (hyper-GT) that emphasizes sustainable performance and advanced technology. Set to begin deliveries in March 2024, the Emeya features rapid acceleration and supercharging capabilities, appealing to eco-conscious drivers who prioritize both luxury and performance. With plans for additional electric models, including a D-segment SUV in 2025 and a BEV sports car in 2026, Lotus is committed to continuous innovation and sustainability in the automotive industry.

The brand's future models will focus on adapting to evolving societal needs, emphasizing sustainability, digital transformation, and user-centric design. By embracing collaboration and change, Lotus aims to remain competitive and relevant in an ever-evolving market, setting benchmarks for future electric vehicles while maintaining its heritage of excellence in engineering and design.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Emeya Emeya (Type 133), a four-door luxury and performance vehicle, is Lotus Technology's second lifestyle vehicle and first hyper grand tourer (hyper-GT) vehicle. Emeya is also one of the world’s most advanced electric hyper-GT vehicles. Lotus Technology commenced deliveries of the Emeya in China in March 2024 and expects to commence deliveries in Europe in the second half of 2024. The average MSRP of the Emeya exceeds US$100,000. Future Models

metadata.title_summary:
title: Eletre 
summary: The Lotus Eletre and Emeya represent the brand's commitment to innovative electric vehicle design, combining luxury, performance, and sustainability. The Eletre is a pioneering pure electric SUV that sets a new standard for lifestyle vehicles, featuring a spacious interior for five passengers, customizable driving modes, and three distinct versions—Eletre, Eletre S, and Eletre R—catering to different customer preferences. The Eletre models are equipped with advanced powertrains, impressive ranges, and rapid charging capabilities, alongside a focus on aerodynamics and luxurious interiors enhanced by cutting-edge technology, including a sophisticated infotainment system and 5G connectivity.

In contrast, the Emeya is Lotus's first hyper grand tourer, showcasing a sleek design and high-performance metrics, aimed at redefining the electric vehicle experience. Deliveries of the Emeya began in China in March 2024, with plans for European deliveries later that year, and it also carries a price point exceeding $100,000. Both models reflect Lotus's dedication to eco-friendly practices and advanced technology, while the broader vision for future models emphasizes the integration of sustainable practices and smart technologies to address urban challenges and enhance quality of life.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Pursuant to the requirements of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, Lotus Technology has duly caused this report to be signed on its behalf by the undersigned, thereunto duly authorized. NEW YORK, Jan. 14, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus” or the “Company”), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its preliminary vehicle delivery results for the full year of 2024. Lotus Technology delivered 12,065 vehicles in 2024, representing a year-on-year increase of over 70% and boasting the top growth rate among leading luxury brands. China contributed approximately 25% of the total global deliveries in 2024. In 2025, Lotus Technology aims to increase global deliveries by 20% with a 25-28% contribution from China. Lotus Technology also successfully reclaimed the proprietary rights of the LOTUS roundel, LOTUS word mark, and the Chinese character of Lotus ("莲花") in China, achieving consistency in terms of its trademark across the world. Note 1: Invoiced deliveries, including commissioned deliveries in the US market. The vehicle delivery numbers presented above are derived from reports submitted by Lotus Technology's business units. These numbers may differ slightly from the vehicle delivery numbers recorded in the financial accounting and reporting systems announced or to be announced by Lotus Technology. Note 2: Including Ferrari, Porsche, Aston Martin, Bentley, Rolls Royce, and McLaren.

This performance established Lotus as the fastest-growing luxury brand, with approximately 25% of deliveries originating from China. Looking ahead, Lotus aims to increase global deliveries by 20% in 2025, with China's contribution expected to rise to 25-28%. The company has also regained proprietary rights to its trademarks, including the iconic LOTUS roundel and the Chinese character for Lotus ("莲花"), reinforcing its global branding consistency. The reported delivery figures encompass invoiced and commissioned deliveries in the US, based on submissions from various business units, and may differ slightly from the company's financial records. metadata.title_summary:
title: Our Business 
summary: **Comprehensive Summary of Our Business**

Lotus is a premier global provider of intelligent and luxury mobility solutions, renowned for designing and developing luxury lifestyle vehicles under its iconic British brand. Established in the U.K. in 1948, Lotus has a rich heritage of over seven decades in racing, symbolizing excellence in performance, design, and engineering, with a legacy that includes 13 FIA Formula 1 world titles.

In 2017, Geely Holding acquired a 51% stake in Lotus UK, transforming the company into a luxury lifestyle battery electric vehicle (BEV) manufacturer. This strategic partnership enhances Lotus's capabilities in manufacturing, supply chain, research and development (R&D), and logistics, facilitating a transition from a traditional sports car company to a leader in high-performance electric vehicles. The luxury BEV market is currently underserved, with only about 10 existing models compared to over 100 internal combustion engine luxury models, positioning Lotus to capitalize on first-mover advantages.

Lotus launched its first fully electric Hyper-SUV, the Eletre, in 2022, with deliveries commencing in 2023. The Eletre features an advanced 800-volt EPA platform, offering three versions: Eletre, Eletre S, and Eletre R. The Eletre R achieves an impressive 905 horsepower and accelerates from 0-100 km/h in just 2.95 seconds. Its 112-kWh battery provides a WLTP range of 490 km and can be charged from 10% to 80% in under 20 minutes, with an average MSRP exceeding $100,000. Following the Eletre, the second model, the Emeya, was launched in September 2023, with deliveries expected to begin in March 2024.

Lotus's R&D capabilities are a competitive strength, highlighted by the proprietary 800-volt EPA developed over five years, which supports rapid model rollout and production scalability. The company also features advanced ADAS technology, including the first deployable LiDAR system. R&D facilities in Europe and China foster collaboration among experienced teams, enhancing innovation.

All BEV lifestyle models are manufactured through a contract partnership with Geely, utilizing their state-of-the-art EV manufacturing facilities in Wuhan, China. This partnership enhances scalability and flexibility while minimizing upfront capital commitments. Access to Geely’s global supply chain allows Lotus to secure high-quality components at competitive prices, effectively managing supply chain risks.

Lotus's sales strategy emphasizes a digital-first, omni-channel approach, providing a luxury retail experience through premium stores and a comprehensive digital platform. As of June 30, 2024, the company has established a global distribution network with 207 stores and a Global Commercial Platform for distributing Lotus vehicles, including the Eletre and Emeya. In 2023, Lotus delivered 6,970 vehicles, with 4,873 vehicles delivered in the first half of 2024, encompassing both lifestyle and sports cars.

This comprehensive approach positions Lotus as a leader in the luxury BEV market, leveraging its rich heritage, innovative technology, and strategic partnerships to drive future growth.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Operating Highlights for the First Half of
2024 
summary: In the first half of 2024, the Company achieved a remarkable 239% year-on-year increase in total deliveries, reaching 4,873 units, with significant contributions from various global regions, each accounting for 20-30% of the total. The US market notably represented 26% of these deliveries, reflecting the Company's successful re-entry into the market. 

Deliveries were categorized by model type, with Lifestyle SUVs and Sedans totaling 2,389 units (up 174% from 2023) and Sportscars reaching 2,484 units (a 337% increase). Regionally, Europe led with 1,459 deliveries (30% of total), followed by North America with 1,278 (26%), China with 1,208 (25%), and the Rest of the World with 928 (19%).

The Company is evolving from a traditional sports car manufacturer to an intelligent luxury mobility provider, focusing on technology-related revenue through intelligent driving solutions and R&D services. The introduction of the Chapman Bespoke service caters to growing customer demand for personalized designs, enhancing the brand's luxury appeal and potentially improving long-term gross margins.

In light of changing market conditions and uncertainties related to new tariff policies, the Company has revised its 2024 delivery target to 12,000 units. Additionally, the "Win26" plan has been launched to achieve positive EBITDA by 2026, emphasizing process optimization, cost-saving measures, and product strategy recalibration to better meet the needs of diverse global markets.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: New York – November 12, 2024 – Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus” or the “Company”), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its vehicle delivery results for the first ten months of 2024. Lotus Technology Inc. continues to achieve top-rate growth among traditional luxury car brands, delivering 8,631 vehicles in the first ten months of 2024. Europe and China continued to be the major markets for Lotus Technology, with each region contributing about 35% and 25% of the total deliveries, respectively. In October, Lotus Technology Inc. started an open beta test for its urban NOA (Navigate on Autopilot) in key cities. Certain over-the-air updates of intelligent driving, including highway assistant driving functions, were launched in Europe. As previously announced, Lotus Robotics, Lotus Technology's intelligent driving arm, provides intelligent driving solutions with global coverage to both Lotus-brand intelligent vehicles and other global leading auto conglomerates such as Lynk&Co, Farizon Auto, and a top Japanese Tier-one supplier. In October, Lotus Technology Inc. was awarded the Highly Commends honor at the Reuters Sustainability Awards 2024 in the Business Transformation category as the only awardee from the automotive industry. Note 1: Invoiced deliveries, including commissioned deliveries in the US market.

As of June 30, 2024, our global distribution network comprises 207 stores. In 2023, we delivered a total of 6,970 vehicles, with 4,873 vehicles delivered in the first half of 2024. Our workforce consists of 2,989 full-time employees, with a substantial 54.6% dedicated to research and development. 

This comprehensive approach positions us at the forefront of the luxury electric vehicle market, driving innovation and excellence in mobility solutions.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Overview 
summary: **Comprehensive Summary of the Overview Chapter**

We are a pioneering luxury battery electric vehicle (BEV) manufacturer operating under the iconic British brand "Lotus," which boasts over 70 years of racing heritage. Since our acquisition by Geely Holding in 2017, we have been transitioning from a traditional sports car company to a luxury lifestyle BEV maker, leveraging Geely's extensive support in manufacturing, supply chain, and research and development (R&D).

The global luxury BEV market, defined as vehicles with a Manufacturer's Suggested Retail Price (MSRP) exceeding $80,000, is projected to experience significant growth, with a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 35% from 2021 to 2031, ultimately reaching nearly 1.9 million units by 2031. Currently, this market is underserved, featuring only about 10 luxury BEV models compared to over 100 internal combustion engine (ICE) luxury models.

Our first fully electric model, the Eletre, launched in 2022, is a luxury E-segment SUV powered by our proprietary 800-volt Electric Performance Architecture (EPA). The Eletre is available in three versions, with the Eletre R delivering an impressive 905 horsepower and achieving 0 to 100 km/h in just 2.95 seconds. We aim to achieve a 100% BEV product portfolio by 2027, having launched our second lifestyle vehicle, the Emeya, in September 2023, with deliveries scheduled to commence in March 2024. Future product launches include a D-segment SUV in 2025 and a BEV lifestyle sports car in 2026.

Our R&D capabilities serve as a competitive strength, supported by five facilities located in the U.K., Germany, and China. These facilities focus on developing advanced technologies, including a leading Advanced Driver Assistance System (ADAS). We manufacture our BEV models in partnership with Geely, utilizing their state-of-the-art facilities in Wuhan, China, with a planned annual production capacity of 150,000 units.

We employ a digital-first, omni-channel sales model, operating 215 stores globally as of December 31, 2023, with plans to expand to over 300 by 2025. In 2023, we successfully delivered 6,970 vehicles, encompassing both lifestyle and sports car models. Our collaboration with L Catterton, which has connections to LVMH, is anticipated to enhance our brand awareness and consumer insights, although no formal agreements with LVMH have been finalized as of this prospectus.

This strategic positioning within the luxury BEV market, combined with our innovative product offerings and robust R&D capabilities, positions us well for future growth and success in the evolving automotive landscape.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: New York – Oct 17, 2024 – Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus” or the “Company”), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its preliminary vehicle delivery results for the first nine months of 2024. Lotus Technology Inc. continues to achieve top-rate growth among traditional luxury car brands, delivering 7,543 vehicles in the first nine months of 2024, representing a year-on-year increase of 134%. Global allocation in terms of deliveries remained well-distributed, with Europe, China, North America, and the Rest of the World regions each contributing about 20-35% of the total deliveries. In the third quarter of 2024, the Emeya (GT, Sedan) was delivered to Europe, whilst the Eletre (SUV) reached markets including the GCC, Japan, and Korea. In September, Lotus Technology Inc. revealed the Eletre Carbon, a high-performance variant tailored for the North American market with a starting price of $229,900. The deliveries of the Eletre Carbon are expected to commence in the first half of 2025. Deliveries of bespoke and collection versions of both the Eletre (SUV) and the Emeya (GT, Sedan) also commenced in the third quarter.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Lotus Technology Announces Delivery Updates
of First Nine Months of 2024 
summary: Lotus Technology Inc. announced its preliminary vehicle delivery results for the first nine months of 2024 on October 17, 2024, reporting a total of 7,543 vehicles delivered, which represents a remarkable 134% increase compared to the same period last year. The deliveries were well-distributed across global markets, with Europe, China, North America, and the Rest of the World each contributing approximately 20-35% to the overall delivery figures.

In the third quarter of 2024, the company successfully delivered the Emeya (GT, Sedan) in Europe, while the Eletre (SUV) expanded its reach to markets including the Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC), Japan, and Korea. Notably, the Eletre Carbon, a high-performance variant specifically designed for the North American market, was revealed in September 2024, with a starting price set at $229,900. Deliveries for this model are anticipated to commence in the first half of 2025. Additionally, bespoke and collection versions of both the Eletre and Emeya began their deliveries in Q3 2024.

Lotus also showcased the Theory 1 concept car in September, highlighting its commitment to advanced technology and eco-friendly features. It is important to note that the delivery figures provided are based on reports from business units and may differ from those recorded in financial accounting systems. This comprehensive overview underscores Lotus Technology's significant growth trajectory and its strategic expansion into various international markets.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Operational and Financial Summaries 
summary: The operational results for Lotus Tech in the first quarter of 2024 (Q1 2024) reveal significant growth across various metrics compared to the same period in 2023 (Q1 2023). The company saw a remarkable increase in vehicle deliveries, with lifestyle vehicle deliveries soaring to 1,047 units—up 344% from 236 units in Q1 2023—and sportscar deliveries reaching 1,147 units, a substantial rise from just 28 units in the previous year. Overall, combined deliveries for both categories totaled 2,194 units, reflecting an impressive 731% year-over-year growth from 264 units in Q1 2023.

Financially, Lotus Tech reported a revenue of $173 million for Q1 2024, representing an 811% increase from $19 million in Q1 2023. However, the cost of revenues also increased significantly to $143 million, leading to a gross profit of $30 million and a gross profit margin of 18%, up from 1% in the prior year. Despite these gains, the company faced challenges with an operating loss of $233 million and a net loss of $258 million, both of which widened compared to the previous year. Adjusted EBITDA for Q1 2024 was negative $204 million, indicating a decline in profitability.

These results underscore Lotus Tech's strategic expansion into luxury markets and its commitment to innovation under the Vision80 plan, while also highlighting the ongoing challenges in achieving profitability amidst rapid growth and investment in global market engagement.

Overall, Lotus Technology continues to make significant strides in vehicle delivery, technological advancements, and sustainability initiatives.

summary: Lotus Technology Inc. has released its unaudited financial results for the third quarter and the first nine months of 2024, concluding on September 30, 2024. Overall, Lotus Technology's third-quarter and nine-month results reflect strong growth and innovation within the company, positioning it favorably in the competitive automotive market.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Deliveries by model type 
summary: In Q4 2023, vehicle sales surged to 3,749 units, a remarkable 110% increase from Q3's 1,782 units, primarily driven by a 191% rise in lifestyle vehicle sales, which jumped from 891 to 2,599 units. Sportscar sales also increased by 29%, from 891 units in Q3 to 1,150 in Q4. For the entire year, total vehicle sales reached 6,970 units, with lifestyle vehicles accounting for 4,361 units and sportscars for 2,609 units.

The "Regional Deliveries Comparison" highlights significant growth in deliveries across various regions from December 31, 2022, to December 31, 2023. North America saw deliveries rise from 41 to 47, Europe from 54 to 70, and China from 41 to 65, while deliveries in the Rest of the World remained steady at 33. Overall, total deliveries increased from 169 to 215, showcasing the company's successful expansion efforts.

Financially, Lotus Tech reported substantial growth in revenue and gross profit in 2023, particularly in Q4, where revenue reached $361 million, a 92% increase from Q3's $188 million. The total revenue for the year was $679 million. However, the cost of sales also rose to $293 million in Q4, leading to a gross profit of $68 million, up 142% from Q3. Despite these gains, the company faced significant operating and net losses, with an operating loss of $228 million in Q4 and a total net loss of $750 million for the year. Adjusted EBITDA also worsened, reflecting ongoing challenges in executing its Vision80 strategy amidst financial pressures.

- **Table Summary:** The table displays key financial metrics for Lotus Technology's performance during the first quarter of 2024 (1Q 2024), compared to the first quarter of 2023 (1Q 2023), alongside the percentage changes year-over-year (YoY) and full-year 2023 (FY2023) figures. Key metrics include Revenue, Cost of Revenues, Gross Profit, Operating Loss, Net Loss, and Adjusted EBITDA.  
- **Context:** Lotus Technology reported significant growth in global markets outside China and expanded its sales network across the Middle East, Asia, and the Americas. Continued efforts to innovate and expand internationally are central to the company’s Vision80 plan.  
- **Special Notes:** The Adjusted EBITDA includes a footnote ("*") that may provide additional clarification not visible in the image. All monetary values appear to use the same unit (likely millions). Gross margin is represented as a percentage.  

[Row Level]  
- **Row 1:** In 1Q 2024, Lotus Technology’s revenue was 173 million, a dramatic increase of 811% compared to the 19 million revenue recorded in 1Q 2023. For FY2023, total revenue was 679 million.  
- **Row 2:** The cost of revenues for 1Q 2024 was 143 million, a significant jump of 653% from 19 million in 1Q 2023. For FY2023, cost of revenues totaled 577 million.  
- **Row 3:** Gross profit for 1Q 2024 stood at 30 million, compared to 0.2 million in 1Q 2023. This reflects a gross margin of 18% in 1Q 2024, up from 1% in the same quarter of the previous year. The gross profit for FY2023 was 102 million, with a gross margin of 15%.  
- **Row 4:** Operating loss increased to 233 million in 1Q 2024, representing a 44% rise from 162 million in 1Q 2023. For FY2023, the total operating loss was 736 million.  
- **Row 5:** Net loss for 1Q 2024 was 258 million, up 61% from 160 million in 1Q 2023. The FY2023 net loss amounted to 750 million.  
- **Row 6:** Adjusted EBITDA for 1Q 2024 was -204 million, worsening by 32% compared to -154 million in 1Q 2023. For FY2023, Adjusted EBITDA stood at -693 million.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Operational and Financial Summaries 
summary: The operational results for Lotus Tech for the first quarter of 2024 (1Q 2024) reveal substantial growth across various performance metrics compared to the same period in 2023 (1Q 2023). Key highlights include a dramatic increase in vehicle deliveries, with total units reaching 2,194, marking a 731% rise from 264 units in 1Q 2023. Notably, lifestyle vehicle deliveries surged to 1,047 units, up 344% from 236 units, while sportscar deliveries rose to 1,147 units from just 28 units, reflecting strong demand and customer preferences.

Financially, Lotus Tech reported revenues of 173 million in 1Q 2024, an impressive 811% increase from 19 million in the previous year, with total revenue for FY2023 at 679 million. However, the cost of revenues also escalated to 143 million, a 653% increase, leading to a gross profit of 30 million and a gross margin of 18%. Despite these positive trends, the company faced challenges with an operating loss of 233 million, a 44% increase from the prior year, and a net loss of 258 million, up 61%. Adjusted EBITDA also declined to -204 million.

Overall, while Lotus Tech has demonstrated significant growth in deliveries and revenue, it continues to experience substantial operating and net losses as it advances its Vision80 plan for international expansion and innovation. The performance indicators underscore both the potential and the challenges the organization faces in a competitive market.

metadata.title_summary:
title: 4Q & FY2O23 SUMMARY 
summary: Lotus's fiscal year 2023 performance metrics highlight remarkable growth in vehicle deliveries, revenue, and profit margins, aligning with its Vision80 strategy. The company recorded a total of 6,970 vehicle deliveries, generating $679 million in revenue and achieving a gross profit margin of 15%. 

In the first half of 2023, Lotus delivered 1,439 units, resulting in $130 million in revenue and a 5% gross profit margin. This momentum continued into the third quarter, with deliveries increasing to 1,782 units and revenue rising to $188 million, while maintaining a 15% gross profit margin. The fourth quarter saw a significant surge, with 3,749 units delivered, leading to $361 million in revenue and a gross profit margin of 19%.

These achievements reflect Lotus's dedication to boosting its battery electric vehicle (BEV) sales, expanding its global retail footprint, and redefining luxury within the automotive industry. All financial figures are unaudited, and "Lotus Tech" refers to Lotus Technology Inc. and its subsidiaries.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Emeya (Type 133), a four-door luxury and performance vehicle, is Lotus Technology's second lifestyle vehicle and first hyper grand tourer (hyper-GT) vehicle. The Emeya is also one of the world’s most advanced electric hyper-GT vehicles. Lotus Technology expects to start production of the Emeya in 2024. The average MSRP will be higher than US$100,000. The annual sales volume of the Emeya is expected to be between 30,000 and 40,000 units starting from 2027. Future Models Type 134 Type 134 is a D-segment battery electric vehicle (BEV) SUV planned for launch in 2024 with expected delivery in 2026. With an average MSRP higher than US$70,000, Type 134 will be an SUV targeting younger demographics. The annual sales volume of Type 134 is expected to be between 80,000 and 90,000 units starting from 2027. Type 135 Type 135 is a BEV sports car planned for launch in 2025 with expected delivery in 2027. With an average MSRP higher than US$95,000, Type 135 will be Lotus Technology's first all-electric sports car, built upon Lotus’s unique sports car platform. The expected annual sales volume of Type 135 is projected to be between 10,000 and 15,000 units starting from 2029.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Emeya 
summary: The Emeya (Type 133) is a luxury four-door hyper grand tourer (hyper-GT) that showcases advanced electric vehicle technology, high-end design, and performance, with production starting in 2024 and an expected MSRP over $100,000. Annual sales are projected to reach 30,000 to 40,000 units by 2027. In addition, the Type 134 is a conceptual D-segment battery electric SUV set to launch in 2024, targeting younger consumers with an anticipated MSRP exceeding $70,000 and projected annual sales of 80,000 to 90,000 units starting in 2027. Lastly, the Type 135 is an all-electric sports car, Lotus's first BEV sports model, expected to launch in 2025 with deliveries in 2027, priced above $95,000 and projected to sell between 10,000 and 15,000 units annually starting in 2029.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: The projected delivery volumes that were used to prepare the October Updated Projections are summarized as follows: • Lotus Technology anticipated sales of approximately 9,000 vehicles in 2023, including approximately 6,000 units of the Eletre and approximately 3,000 units of sports car models, the Emira and Evija. It was also anticipated that in 2023, China, Europe, and the United States would contribute approximately 55%, 30%, and 3% of the total sales volume respectively (with the rest of the world, including Asia (other than China), Australia, certain parts of the Middle East, and South America, contributing approximately 12% of the total sales volume). • Lotus Technology anticipated sales of approximately 47,000 vehicles in 2024, including approximately 29,500 units of the Eletre, approximately 11,500 units of the Emeya, and approximately 6,000 units of sports car models. It was also anticipated that in 2024, China, Europe, and the United States would contribute approximately 38%, 32%, and 19% of the total sales volume respectively (with the rest of the world, including Asia (other than China), Australia, certain parts of the Middle East, and South America, contributing approximately 11% of the total sales volume). • Lotus Technology anticipated sales of over 73,000 vehicles in 2025, including approximately 42,300 units of the Eletre, 24,700 units of the Emeya, and approximately 6,000 units of sports car models.

metadata.title_summary:
title: •  Projected vehicle related revenue: 
summary: Lotus Tech is projecting significant growth in vehicle sales, anticipating to exceed 73,000 units by 2025, which represents a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 185% from 2023 to 2025. This growth is primarily driven by the sales of its models, including the Eletre, Emeya, Emira, and Evija. The company expects to sell approximately 9,000 vehicles in 2023, with a notable sales distribution from China (55%), Europe (30%), and the US (3%). By 2024, sales are projected to rise to around 47,000 vehicles, and by 2025, to over 73,000, with a more balanced contribution from key markets.

Financially, Lotus Tech anticipates a rise in gross profit margins from an estimated 8%–10% in 2023 to 21%–23% by 2025, despite a downward revision in initial gross margin estimates due to increased raw material costs. The company also expects to achieve a positive EBITDA margin exceeding 4% by 2025, driven by improved gross profit margins and reduced operating expenses. However, the company faces challenges, including potential delays in vehicle deliveries due to customs issues and the European Commission's anti-subsidy investigation affecting pricing strategies in Europe.

Lotus Tech's financial projections include securing $223 million from the Trust Account and an additional $500 million through fundraising, but there are risks associated with meeting these financial targets. The company has indicated a cash outflow exceeding $1 billion from operating and investing activities in 2023, highlighting the need for careful financial management moving forward.

Overall, Lotus Tech's projections reflect a strong growth trajectory in the luxury battery electric vehicle market, with a focus on expanding sales and improving profitability, while also navigating potential market and operational challenges.

It was also anticipated that in 2023, China, Europe, and the United States would contribute approximately 55%, 30%, and 3% of the total sales volume respectively (with the rest of the world, including Asia (other than China), Australia, certain parts of the Middle East, and South America, contributing approximately 12% of the total sales volume). • Lotus Technology anticipated sales of approximately 47,000 vehicles in 2024, including approximately 29,500 units of the Eletre, approximately 11,500 units of the Emeya, and approximately 6,000 units of the sports car models. It was also anticipated that in 2024, China, Europe, and the United States would contribute approximately 38%, 32%, and 19% of the total sales volume respectively (with the rest of the world, including Asia (other than China), Australia, certain parts of the Middle East, and South America, contributing approximately 11% of the total sales volume). • Lotus Technology anticipated sales of over 73,000 vehicles in 2025, including approximately 42,300 units of the Eletre, 24,700 units of the Emeya, and approximately 6,000 units of the sports car models.

metadata.title_summary:
title: •  Projected vehicle related revenue: 
summary: Lotus Tech is projecting significant growth in vehicle sales, expecting to exceed 73,000 units by 2025, which translates to a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 185% from 2023 to 2025. This growth is primarily driven by the sales of its models, including the Eletre, Emeya, Emira, and Evija. The company's sales forecasts for 2023 estimate around 9,000 vehicles, with the Eletre model contributing approximately 6,000 units. By 2024, sales are expected to rise to about 47,000 vehicles, and by 2025, they are projected to surpass 73,000 units, with a notable increase in the contribution from the Eletre and Emeya models.

The anticipated sales distribution highlights a strong focus on markets in China, Europe, and the United States, with China expected to account for a significant portion of sales. The company also plans to adjust pricing for its models in Europe due to potential tariff increases linked to an anti-subsidy investigation into Chinese battery electric vehicle (BEV) imports.

Financially, Lotus Tech expects revenues to grow from an estimated $0.9 billion to $1.2 billion in 2023, reaching between $8.2 billion and $8.6 billion by 2025. Gross profit is projected to increase from $0.1 billion to $0.2 billion in 2023 to between $1.7 billion and $1.9 billion in 2025, with gross margins improving from 8%-10% in 2023 to 21%-23% by 2025. Despite some challenges, including potential delivery delays and increased raw material costs, the company anticipates achieving a positive EBITDA margin exceeding 4% by 2025.

Lotus Tech's projections are based on several assumptions regarding market potential, model launch timelines, and the effectiveness of its sales network. However, the company acknowledges the inherent uncertainties in these forecasts and advises caution in relying solely on the projected figures. Overall, Lotus Tech's outlook reflects a robust growth trajectory in the luxury BEV segment, driven by strong demand for its innovative vehicle offerings.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: The projected delivery volumes that were used to prepare the Initial Projections are summarized as follows: • Lotus Technology anticipated sales of approximately 21,500 vehicles in 2023, including approximately 18,000 units of the Eletre and approximately 3,500 units of the sports car models, Emira and Evija. It was also anticipated that in 2023, China, the UK, the EU, and the US would contribute approximately 46%, 22%, 28%, and 1% of the total sales volume respectively (with the rest of the world, including Asia (other than China), Australia, certain parts of the Middle East, and South America, contributing approximately 2% of the total sales volume). • Lotus Technology anticipated sales of approximately 55,500 vehicles in 2024, including approximately 28,000 units of the Eletre, approximately 22,000 units of Type 133, and approximately 5,500 units of the sports car models. It was also anticipated that in 2024, China, the UK, the EU, and the US would contribute approximately 43%, 18%, 23%, and 9% of the total sales volume respectively (with the rest of the world, including Asia (other than China), Australia, certain parts of the Middle East, and South America, contributing approximately 6% of the total sales volume). • Lotus Technology anticipated sales of over 76,000 vehicles in 2025, including approximately 41,000 units of the Eletre, 29,000 units of Type 133, and approximately 6,000 units of the sports car models.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Certain Prospective Operational and Financial Information 
summary: On November 28, 2022, Lotus Tech provided LCAA with initial financial forecasts covering revenue, gross profit, and EBITDA margin estimates for 2023 to 2025, which were prepared without adherence to SEC guidelines or U.S. GAAP. These projections were intended for internal use to assist LCAA's due diligence prior to the Business Combination approval. Subsequent updates were shared in June and October 2023, collectively referred to as the "Financial Projections." 

Lotus Tech emphasizes that these projections are subjective and based on various assumptions, many of which are uncertain and may change over time, potentially leading to significant discrepancies between projected and actual results. The company does not publicly disclose such forecasts and does not intend to do so in the future, highlighting that the projections should not be relied upon as guarantees of future performance.

The Financial Projections indicate a growth trajectory, with revenues expected to rise from approximately $2.2 billion to $2.5 billion in 2023, reaching between $6.2 billion and $6.6 billion in 2024, and further increasing to between $8.5 billion and $8.9 billion in 2025. Gross profit is projected to grow from $0.3 billion to $0.4 billion in 2023 to between $1.8 billion and $2.0 billion by 2025, with gross margins improving from 14%–16% to 21%–23% over the same period.

Lotus Tech anticipates vehicle sales will exceed 76,000 units by 2025, driven by models such as the Eletre and Type 133, with significant contributions from both vehicle-related and technology-related revenues. The company also projects growth in ecosystem-related revenue through charging services and peripheral product sales. However, stakeholders are cautioned about the inherent risks and uncertainties that could impact these forecasts, including market conditions, competitive pressures, and operational challenges.

Overall, while Lotus Tech's management has outlined optimistic projections based on reasonable assumptions, the company acknowledges the potential for actual results to vary significantly from these estimates, underscoring the need for caution among investors and stakeholders.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Launched in 2021, the Emira sports car is built on a new Lotus sports car architecture. The Emira utilizes the pioneering Lotus bonded extruded aluminum chassis technology. The Emira measures 4,412 millimeters in length with a 2,575-millimeter wheelbase. The Emira comes with a power output of 298 kilowatts. The Emira can deliver a top speed of 290 km per hour. Acceleration from zero to 100 km per hour in 4.5 seconds, with a maximum of 420 Nm of torque. With an average MSRP higher than US$85,000, the annual sales volume of the Emira sports car is expected to be between 5,000 to 6,000 units starting from 2024.

title: Exhibit No.   Description 
summary: Lotus Technology has revealed promising preliminary delivery results for 2024, showcasing a notable rise in vehicle deliveries driven by robust demand for its electric vehicles. all_retrieved_content.page_content: Pursuant to the requirements of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, Lotus Technology has duly caused this report to be signed on its behalf by the undersigned, thereunto duly authorized. NEW YORK, Jan. 14, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus” or the “Company”), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its preliminary vehicle delivery results for the full year of 2024. Lotus Technology delivered 12,065 vehicles in 2024, representing a year-on-year increase of over 70% and boasting the top growth rate among leading luxury brands. China contributed approximately 25% of the total global deliveries in 2024. In 2025, Lotus Technology aims to increase global deliveries by 20% with a 25-28% contribution from China. Lotus Technology also successfully reclaimed the proprietary rights of the LOTUS roundel, LOTUS word mark, and the Chinese character of Lotus ("莲花") in China, achieving consistency in terms of its trademark across the world. Note 1: Invoiced deliveries, including commissioned deliveries in the US market. The vehicle delivery numbers presented above are derived from reports submitted by Lotus Technology's business units. These numbers may differ slightly from the vehicle delivery numbers recorded in the financial accounting and reporting systems announced or to be announced by Lotus Technology. Note 2: Including Ferrari, Porsche, Aston Martin, Bentley, Rolls Royce, and McLaren.

metadata.title_summary:
title: SIGNATURES 
summary: Lotus Technology Inc. has filed a report in accordance with the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, officially signed by an authorized representative. On January 14, 2025, the company announced its preliminary vehicle delivery results for 2024, achieving a significant milestone with 12,065 vehicles delivered, reflecting over 70% year-on-year growth. This performance established Lotus as the fastest-growing luxury brand, with approximately 25% of deliveries originating from China. Looking ahead, Lotus aims to increase global deliveries by 20% in 2025, with China's contribution expected to rise to 25-28%. The company has also regained proprietary rights to its trademarks, including the iconic LOTUS roundel and the Chinese character for Lotus ("莲花"), reinforcing its global branding consistency. metadata.title_summary:
title: Our Business 
summary: **Comprehensive Summary of Our Business**

Lotus is a premier global provider of intelligent and luxury mobility solutions, renowned for designing and developing luxury lifestyle vehicles under its iconic British brand. Established in the U.K. in 1948, Lotus has a rich heritage of over seven decades in racing, symbolizing excellence in performance, design, and engineering, with a legacy that includes 13 FIA Formula 1 world titles.

In 2017, Geely Holding acquired a 51% stake in Lotus UK, transforming the company into a luxury lifestyle battery electric vehicle (BEV) manufacturer. This strategic partnership enhances Lotus's capabilities in manufacturing, supply chain, research and development (R&D), and logistics, facilitating a transition from a traditional sports car company to a leader in high-performance electric vehicles. The luxury BEV market is currently underserved, with only about 10 existing models compared to over 100 internal combustion engine luxury models, positioning Lotus to capitalize on first-mover advantages.

Lotus launched its first fully electric Hyper-SUV, the Eletre, in 2022, with deliveries commencing in 2023. The Eletre features an advanced 800-volt EPA platform, offering three versions: Eletre, Eletre S, and Eletre R. The Eletre R achieves an impressive 905 horsepower and accelerates from 0-100 km/h in just 2.95 seconds. Its 112-kWh battery provides a WLTP range of 490 km and can be charged from 10% to 80% in under 20 minutes, with an average MSRP exceeding $100,000. Following the Eletre, the second model, the Emeya, was launched in September 2023, with deliveries expected to begin in March 2024.

Lotus's R&D capabilities are a competitive strength, highlighted by the proprietary 800-volt EPA developed over five years, which supports rapid model rollout and production scalability. The company also features advanced ADAS technology, including the first deployable LiDAR system. R&D facilities in Europe and China foster collaboration among experienced teams, enhancing innovation.

All BEV lifestyle models are manufactured through a contract partnership with Geely, utilizing their state-of-the-art EV manufacturing facilities in Wuhan, China. This partnership enhances scalability and flexibility while minimizing upfront capital commitments. Access to Geely’s global supply chain allows Lotus to secure high-quality components at competitive prices, effectively managing supply chain risks.

Lotus's sales strategy emphasizes a digital-first, omni-channel approach, providing a luxury retail experience through premium stores and a comprehensive digital platform. As of June 30, 2024, the company has established a global distribution network with 207 stores and a Global Commercial Platform for distributing Lotus vehicles, including the Eletre and Emeya. In 2023, Lotus delivered 6,970 vehicles, with 4,873 vehicles delivered in the first half of 2024, encompassing both lifestyle and sports cars.

This comprehensive approach positions Lotus as a leader in the luxury BEV market, leveraging its rich heritage, innovative technology, and strategic partnerships to drive future growth.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Overview 
summary: **Comprehensive Summary of the Overview Chapter**

We are a pioneering luxury battery electric vehicle (BEV) manufacturer operating under the iconic British brand "Lotus," which boasts over 70 years of racing heritage. Since our acquisition by Geely Holding in 2017, we have been transitioning from a traditional sports car company to a luxury lifestyle BEV maker, leveraging Geely's extensive support in manufacturing, supply chain, and research and development (R&D).

The global luxury BEV market, defined as vehicles with a Manufacturer's Suggested Retail Price (MSRP) exceeding $80,000, is projected to experience significant growth, with a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 35% from 2021 to 2031, ultimately reaching nearly 1.9 million units by 2031. Currently, this market is underserved, featuring only about 10 luxury BEV models compared to over 100 internal combustion engine (ICE) luxury models.

Our first fully electric model, the Eletre, launched in 2022, is a luxury E-segment SUV powered by our proprietary 800-volt Electric Performance Architecture (EPA). The Eletre is available in three versions, with the Eletre R delivering an impressive 905 horsepower and achieving 0 to 100 km/h in just 2.95 seconds. We aim to achieve a 100% BEV product portfolio by 2027, having launched our second lifestyle vehicle, the Emeya, in September 2023, with deliveries scheduled to commence in March 2024. Future product launches include a D-segment SUV in 2025 and a BEV lifestyle sports car in 2026.

Our R&D capabilities serve as a competitive strength, supported by five facilities located in the U.K., Germany, and China. These facilities focus on developing advanced technologies, including a leading Advanced Driver Assistance System (ADAS). We manufacture our BEV models in partnership with Geely, utilizing their state-of-the-art facilities in Wuhan, China, with a planned annual production capacity of 150,000 units.

We employ a digital-first, omni-channel sales model, operating 215 stores globally as of December 31, 2023, with plans to expand to over 300 by 2025. In 2023, we successfully delivered 6,970 vehicles, encompassing both lifestyle and sports car models. Our collaboration with L Catterton, which has connections to LVMH, is anticipated to enhance our brand awareness and consumer insights, although no formal agreements with LVMH have been finalized as of this prospectus.

This strategic positioning within the luxury BEV market, combined with our innovative product offerings and robust R&D capabilities, positions us well for future growth and success in the evolving automotive landscape.

(“Lotus” or the “Company”), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its vehicle delivery results for the first ten months of 2024. Lotus Technology Inc. continues to achieve top-rate growth among traditional luxury car brands, delivering 8,631 vehicles in the first ten months of 2024. Europe and China continued to be the major markets for Lotus Technology, with each region contributing about 35% and 25% of the total deliveries, respectively. In October, Lotus Technology Inc. started an open beta test for its urban NOA (Navigate on Autopilot) in key cities. Certain over-the-air updates of intelligent driving, including highway assistant driving functions, were launched in Europe. As previously announced, Lotus Robotics, Lotus Technology's intelligent driving arm, provides intelligent driving solutions with global coverage to both Lotus-brand intelligent vehicles and other global leading auto conglomerates such as Lynk&Co, Farizon Auto, and a top Japanese Tier-one supplier. In October, Lotus Technology Inc. was awarded the Highly Commends honor at the Reuters Sustainability Awards 2024 in the Business Transformation category as the only awardee from the automotive industry. Note 1: Invoiced deliveries, including commissioned deliveries in the US market.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: New York – Oct 17, 2024 – Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus” or the “Company”), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its preliminary vehicle delivery results for the first nine months of 2024. Lotus Technology Inc. continues to achieve top-rate growth among traditional luxury car brands, delivering 7,543 vehicles in the first nine months of 2024, representing a year-on-year increase of 134%. Global allocation in terms of deliveries remained well-distributed, with Europe, China, North America, and the Rest of the World regions each contributing about 20-35% of the total deliveries. In the third quarter of 2024, the Emeya (GT, Sedan) was delivered to Europe, whilst the Eletre (SUV) reached markets including the GCC, Japan, and Korea. In September, Lotus Technology Inc. revealed the Eletre Carbon, a high-performance variant tailored for the North American market with a starting price of $229,900. The deliveries of the Eletre Carbon are expected to commence in the first half of 2025. Deliveries of bespoke and collection versions of both the Eletre (SUV) and the Emeya (GT, Sedan) also commenced in the third quarter.

In 2023, Lotus Technology delivered a total of 6,970 vehicles, which consisted of its lifestyle vehicles and sportscars distributed through Lotus Technology International Limited (LTIL). [Row Level]

- **Row 2:** Sportscar deliveries reached 1,147 units in 1Q 2024, up significantly from just 28 units in 1Q 2023. There's no explicit YoY percentage change provided for this category. For FY2023, a total of 2,609 sportscars were delivered.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Operational and Financial Summaries 
summary: The operational results for Lotus Tech in the first quarter of 2024 (Q1 2024) reveal significant growth across various metrics compared to the same period in 2023 (Q1 2023). The company saw a remarkable increase in vehicle deliveries, with lifestyle vehicle deliveries soaring to 1,047 units—up 344% from 236 units in Q1 2023—and sportscar deliveries reaching 1,147 units, a substantial rise from just 28 units in the previous year. Overall, combined deliveries for both categories totaled 2,194 units, reflecting an impressive 731% year-over-year growth from 264 units in Q1 2023.

Financially, Lotus Tech reported a revenue of $173 million for Q1 2024, representing an 811% increase from $19 million in Q1 2023. However, the cost of revenues also increased significantly to $143 million, leading to a gross profit of $30 million and a gross profit margin of 18%, up from 1% in the prior year. Despite these gains, the company faced challenges with an operating loss of $233 million and a net loss of $258 million, both of which widened compared to the previous year. Adjusted EBITDA for Q1 2024 was negative $204 million, indicating a decline in profitability.

These results underscore Lotus Tech's strategic expansion into luxury markets and its commitment to innovation under the Vision80 plan, while also highlighting the ongoing challenges in achieving profitability amidst rapid growth and investment in global market engagement.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Deliveries by model type 
summary: In Q4 2023, vehicle sales surged to 3,749 units, a remarkable 110% increase from Q3's 1,782 units, primarily driven by a 191% rise in lifestyle vehicle sales, which jumped from 891 to 2,599 units. Sportscar sales also increased by 29%, from 891 units in Q3 to 1,150 in Q4. For the entire year, total vehicle sales reached 6,970 units, with lifestyle vehicles accounting for 4,361 units and sportscars for 2,609 units.

The "Regional Deliveries Comparison" highlights significant growth in deliveries across various regions from December 31, 2022, to December 31, 2023. North America saw deliveries rise from 41 to 47, Europe from 54 to 70, and China from 41 to 65, while deliveries in the Rest of the World remained steady at 33. Overall, total deliveries increased from 169 to 215, showcasing the company's successful expansion efforts.

Financially, Lotus Tech reported substantial growth in revenue and gross profit in 2023, particularly in Q4, where revenue reached $361 million, a 92% increase from Q3's $188 million. The total revenue for the year was $679 million. However, the cost of sales also rose to $293 million in Q4, leading to a gross profit of $68 million, up 142% from Q3. Despite these gains, the company faced significant operating and net losses, with an operating loss of $228 million in Q4 and a total net loss of $750 million for the year. Adjusted EBITDA also worsened, reflecting ongoing challenges in executing its Vision80 strategy amidst financial pressures.

title: Deliveries by model type 
summary: In Q4 2023, vehicle sales surged to 3,749 units, a remarkable 110% increase from Q3's 1,782 units, primarily driven by a 191% rise in lifestyle vehicle sales, which jumped from 891 to 2,599 units. Sportscar sales also increased by 29%, from 891 units in Q3 to 1,150 in Q4. For the entire year, total vehicle sales reached 6,970 units, with lifestyle vehicles accounting for 4,361 units and sportscars for 2,609 units.

The "Regional Deliveries Comparison" highlights significant growth in deliveries across various regions from December 31, 2022, to December 31, 2023. North America saw deliveries rise from 41 to 47, Europe from 54 to 70, and China from 41 to 65, while deliveries in the Rest of the World remained steady at 33. Overall, total deliveries increased from 169 to 215, showcasing the company's successful expansion efforts.

Financially, Lotus Tech reported substantial growth in revenue and gross profit in 2023, particularly in Q4, where revenue reached $361 million, a 92% increase from Q3's $188 million. The total revenue for the year was $679 million. However, the cost of sales also rose to $293 million in Q4, leading to a gross profit of $68 million, up 142% from Q3. Despite these gains, the company faced significant operating and net losses, with an operating loss of $228 million in Q4 and a total net loss of $750 million for the year. Adjusted EBITDA also worsened, reflecting ongoing challenges in executing its Vision80 strategy amidst financial pressures.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: New York – January 14, 2025 – Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus” or the “Company”), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its preliminary vehicle delivery results for the full year of 2024. Lotus Technology Inc. delivered 12,065 vehicles in 2024, representing a year-on-year increase of over 70% and boasting the top growth rate among leading luxury brands. China contributed approximately 25% of the total global deliveries in 2024. In 2025, Lotus Technology Inc. aims to increase global deliveries by 20% with a 25-28% contribution from China. Lotus Technology Inc. also successfully reclaimed the proprietary rights of the LOTUS roundel, LOTUS word mark, and the Chinese character of Lotus ("莲花") in China, achieving consistency in terms of its trademark across the world. Note 1: Invoiced deliveries, including commissioned deliveries in the US market. The vehicle delivery numbers presented above are derived from reports submitted by Lotus Technology Inc.'s business units. These numbers may differ slightly from the vehicle delivery numbers recorded in the financial accounting and reporting systems announced or to be announced by Lotus Technology Inc. Note 2: Including Ferrari, Porsche, Aston Martin, Bentley, Rolls Royce, and McLaren.

title: Lotus Technology Announces Delivery Updates
of First Ten Months of 2024 
summary: Lotus Technology Inc. has reported its vehicle delivery results for the first ten months of 2024, achieving a total of 8,631 vehicles delivered. The primary markets driving these deliveries were Europe and China, which accounted for approximately 35% and 25% of total deliveries, respectively. In October, the company launched an open beta test for its urban Navigate on Autopilot (NOA) feature in key cities and introduced several over-the-air (OTA) intelligent driving functions in Europe. 

Additionally, Lotus Robotics, the company's division focused on intelligent driving solutions, is providing services not only for Lotus vehicles but also for other major automotive manufacturers, including Lynk&Co and Farizon Auto. The company has also been recognized for its commitment to sustainability, receiving the Highly Commends honor at the Reuters Sustainability Awards 2024 in the Business Transformation category, making it the only automotive industry recipient of this accolade.

It is important to note that the delivery figures are based on invoiced deliveries, including commissioned deliveries in the U.S. market, and may differ slightly from the company's financial accounting records. Overall, Lotus Technology continues to make significant strides in vehicle delivery, technological advancements, and sustainability initiatives.
